TaskDescription: In this study, each participant completed a single-target EEG signal acquisition task across four different sessions. Each session comprised 12 blocks, and each block consisted of 30 unique single-target stimulus trials presented in random order. The total duration of each trial was 7 s. During the first second, a red cross appeared at the center of the target as a reminder for the participant to concentrate their gaze on the target center. Subsequently, the stimulus began to flash for 5 s, during which the participant was instructed to focus on the target and avoid blinking. The red cross disappeared after the flickering stimulus ceased, and the participant took a brief 1 s break.
